questions.question: What is the main concept used to find sub-game perfect equilibria in finite horizon games?
questions.explanation: Backward induction is a method used to solve finite horizon games by starting from the last stage and working backward to find the sub-game perfect equilibrium.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a finite horizon game that can be solved using backward induction?
questions.explanation: The Ultimatum Game is a classic example of a finite horizon game that can be solved using backward induction.

questions.question: What is the main assumption required for backward induction to work?
options: Both players have the same preferences
Both players have the same information sets
questions.explanation: Backward induction requires that both players have rational expectations about the actions of the other player.

questions.question: What is the outcome of a sub-game perfect equilibrium in a finite horizon game?
options: The players will play a mixed strategy
The players will play a pure strategy
The players will play a Nash equilibrium
The players will achieve a Pareto optimal outcome
questions.explanation: A sub-game perfect equilibrium in a finite horizon game is a pure strategy equilibrium that achieves the best possible outcome for each player.

questions.question: Who is credited with the development of backward induction as a method for solving finite horizon games?
questions.explanation: John von Neumann is credited with the development of backward induction as a method for solving finite horizon games.
